title: Review round 1: jmap-chat enum-wiring epic
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — one outbound-footgun (PushUrgency::Unknown silently serializes as invalid wire value in ChatPushConfig), one discoverability gap (EndpointType not re-exported), one doc consistency gap (new Unknown variants lack the established round-trip-loss disclaimer). No structural API design problems; all issues are fixable without semver breaks.
Confidence: 85% chance of no missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) whether any of the six serde(other) enums (ChatKind, DeliveryState, ChatMemberRole, ChannelPermissionTargetType, ContactPresence, OwnerPresence) are ever serialized back to the server in practice — if any code path does that, t6n1.7 is a real bug not just a polish issue; (2) whether PushUrgency::Unknown can realistically enter a ChatPushConfig via any caller path not covered by t6n1.16/t6n1.2. Would raise confidence if: QuotaScope is confirmed never outbound (it is JMAP Quota data, inbound-only from server); ChatKind/DeliveryState/etc. are confirmed never set via partial-update methods that re-serialize them. Running out of real complaints: yes — P0/P1/P2 are all solid; P3/P4 are the bottom of the barrel (naming conventions, doc comments, boilerplate), no new correctness issues remain.
